filename_collision_note: monitor/analyst/expansions/EXP-296.json (1KB stub from 2026-05-09T09:17Z) is a pre-existing draft that mistargeted WIN-043 — superseded by this EXP-296-WIN-066-defense.json. Decider should consult JSON body item_id, not filename, per prior analyst run pattern (EXP-295-WIN-063-defense convention).

patches.target_path: WIN-066.detail_verdict_text — final sentence (direction-match non-discriminating) is expanded into a labeled "Defender pivot anticipated" paragraph that addresses both (a) sign-non-discrimination AND (b) the magnitude-disclaim defense.
patches.uniqueness_verified: Substring count = 1 in current data/wins.json:WIN-066.detail_verdict_text (verified 2026-05-09 against workspace head). The find string is the entire final sentence of detail_verdict_text (length 220), occurring exactly once.
patches.find: Note also that the SIGN of the asymmetry (NH > SH) is independently predicted by standard climate science from differential land-mass distribution and aerosol forcing — so getting the direction right is non-discriminating evidence for the dome model.
patches.replace: Defender pivot anticipated. A dome advocate may concede the geometry produces a real center-rim flux gradient and pivot to: "we predict the SIGN (NH > SH); the 90–270 W/m² magnitude is your imputation, not ours; sign-match is meaningful confirmation." Three counters preempt this. (1) The SIGN of the asymmetry (NH > SH) is independently predicted by standard climate science from differential land-mass distribution (~39% NH vs ~19% SH), albedo asymmetry, and time-varying aerosol/cloud forcing — and the actual Loeb et al. 2025 abstract attributes the trend specifically to "changes in hemispheric differences in aerosol-radiation interactions, surface albedo, and water vapor changes." A sign-match against an independent baseline that gets the same sign through different mechanisms is non-discriminating. (2) The dome cites a specific value (+0.34 W/m²/decade) as confirmation, not "any positive NH-SH difference." Sign-only predictions cannot single out a particular number as confirmation; the +0.34 coincidence is post-hoc adoption from CERES literature, not a derivation. (3) Disclaiming the inverse-cube integral that follows from the dome's own h=5,733 km geometry leaves the model with no quantitative prediction at all — a sign-only model with a 50/50 NH > SH-vs-SH > NH prior is equivalent to a coin flip for falsification. Either the dome predicts a magnitude (and is wrong by ~250–800×), or it predicts only sign (and any positive value would have confirmed, making the +0.34 coincidence content-free).
patches.rationale: ISS-1917 defender pivot has two prongs: (a) "sign-match (NH>SH) is what a dome predicts; direction is meaningful" and (b) "we never claimed the 90–270 W/m² magnitude — that's your imputation." The current text has (a) addressed in a single trailing sentence (the c3 hole #4 minor recommendation) but does NOT explicitly preempt (b). The c3 review's hole #2 (TLDR/DETAIL TENSION) was already addressed by prior patches (TLDRs now lead with the over-prediction punchline). What remains unhandled is the "we don't claim magnitude" retreat — formally addressed here by labeling the defender pivot, then refuting it in three numbered counters. The label ("Defender pivot anticipated") matches the convention used in EXP-295 (WIN-063, P3) for explicit defender-anticipation paragraphs. Counters: (1) sign is independently predicted by standard climate science from land-mass and aerosols (citing Loeb et al. 2025's own attribution mechanism — strengthens the c3 hole #4 sentence by quoting the abstract's mechanism); (2) the dome actually cites +0.34 W/m²/decade as confirmation, so the "sign-only" defense contradicts the dome's own quantitative claim; (3) the dome cannot have it both ways — either it derives magnitude (and is wrong by ~250–800×) or it doesn't (and the specific-value coincidence is content-free).

cross_page_consistency_after_patches.docs_index_html: WIN-066 in walkthrough is generated from data/wins.json by build.js. Build.js will regenerate index.html and pick up the new detail_verdict_text. No separate edit needed.
cross_page_consistency_after_patches.docs_dashboard_html: Dashboard does not contain WIN-066 detail prose (only the WIN list / kill-shot summary). No cross-page edit needed.
cross_page_consistency_after_patches.tldr_alignment: tldr_evidence and tldr_verdict already lead with the 90–270 W/m² over-prediction punchline (c3 hole #2 closed prior to this expansion). The new defender-pivot paragraph in detail_verdict_text is consistent with both TLDRs — it explicitly explains why sign-only retreat doesn't save the claim. No TLDR change required.

holes_addressed.hole_summary: Defender pivot: "we concede geometry is asymmetric and a center-rim gradient exists; we predict SIGN (NH>SH); your 90–270 W/m² is your imputation; sign-match is meaningful confirmation."
holes_addressed.how_addressed: P1 expands the existing single-sentence sign-non-discrimination clause into a labeled three-part defender-pivot rebuttal that closes both (a) sign-non-discrimination and (b) the magnitude-disclaim retreat. Counter (3) specifically traps the defender: the dome cited a specific value (+0.34 W/m²/decade) as confirmation, so disclaiming magnitude defeats their own confirmation claim.

holes_not_addressed.hole_summary: Sign-non-discrimination as a SYSTEMIC issue across WIN-058/-059/-060 (NMP/SAA drift) and other directional-match WINs.
holes_not_addressed.reason: Cross-WIN systemic patch is out of scope for a per-WIN defense expansion. Should be raised separately as either (a) a Section 4 (Axial Symmetry Paradox) addition naming sign-match-non-discrimination as a recurring failure mode, or (b) a curmudgeon-driven cross-WIN sweep. Flagging here for analyst/decider attention but not patching as part of EXP-296.

new_evidence_added: Loeb et al. 2025 abstract verbatim mechanism quote ("changes in hemispheric differences in aerosol-radiation interactions, surface albedo, and water vapor changes") — strengthens counter (1) by attributing the trend to specifically time-varying anthropogenic and ice-loss effects, which a static dome geometry structurally cannot produce.

anticipated_objections.objection: "You're attacking a strawman — the dome never claimed 90–270 W/m². You imputed that magnitude and then said we're wrong about it."
anticipated_objections.response: Counter (3) in P1 traps this: if the dome's prediction is sign-only, the +0.34 W/m²/decade coincidence is content-free (any positive value would have confirmed). A model that disclaims magnitude cannot single out a numeric value as confirmation. The dome's own framing (citing the specific +0.34 figure as confirmation) commits it to a quantitative claim, which the inverse-cube integral makes inconsistent with its own geometry.

anticipated_objections.objection: "Standard climate science doesn't 'predict' +0.34 either — it observes it. You're holding the dome to a higher standard than mainstream science."
anticipated_objections.response: True that standard science observes rather than first-principles-derives the +0.34 trend. But standard science (Loeb 2025) provides a MECHANISM (aerosol-radiation, ice loss, water vapor) for why the trend is emerging on a 24-year baseline. A static geometry cannot produce a 24-year trend at all. This is a separate failure layer beyond the magnitude critique — flagged in the c3 review's stronger_arguments #2 but out of scope for this expansion's narrow defender-pivot patch. (Worth a future stronger_arguments expansion.)

anticipated_objections.objection: "Center-rim and NH/SH are not the same axis. Mapping inner-half=NH and outer-half=SH is your construction, not the dome's. Your 90–270 W/m² is therefore a strawman."
anticipated_objections.response: Granted that the inner-half/outer-half→NH/SH mapping is one of several possible mappings. But the dome's own claim is specifically a hemispheric (N vs S) asymmetry, and the dome's geometry is genuinely radially symmetric (no other geometric axis to anchor a hemispheric split). If the defender rejects center=NH then they must propose an alternative dome-geometric basis for 'NH' — and there isn't one in the model. The c3 review's quantitative_verification field documents the inverse-cube integration; the alternative is that the dome has no geometric basis for hemispheric asymmetry at all (which collapses to counter (3): no derivation, no specific-value confirmation).

kernel_of_truth.statement: The dome correctly identifies a real and recent finding (Loeb et al. 2025): an emerging +0.34 W/m²/decade NH-SH absorbed-solar-radiation trend. The CERES satellite data are robust. The dome's intuition that 'an asymmetric Earth-system geometry should produce some hemispheric asymmetry' is not crazy on its face — and the dome's geometry IS genuinely asymmetric in a radial sense (NP at center vs ice rim at the edge), so a center-rim flux gradient really does exist.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Three independent layers of failure persist after granting the kernel: (1) the dome contributes no NEW number — it adopts the published +0.34 wholesale and labels it confirmation; (2) the dome's own geometry, taken seriously, predicts a hemispheric flux difference of 90–270 W/m² (~250–800× too large); (3) the value is a 24-year TREND attributed by Loeb to time-varying anthropogenic effects, which a static dome geometry structurally cannot produce. The defender pivot to 'sign-only prediction' (now preempted in P1) attempts to escape (2) but commits the dome to a content-free claim that any positive NH-SH difference would have confirmed.

test_recommendation: tests/run.sh after build.js html should pass. The patch is prose-only (no DATA blob, no numeric counts, no rates) so existing numeric-claim tests are not affected. Recommend a future test addition: a unit test that detail_verdict_text contains the string 'Defender pivot anticipated' for WIN-066 — guards against silent regressions if the prose is rewritten and the defender-pivot rebuttal is dropped.

closure_recommendation_for_decider.closure_note: EXP-296 P1 (find/replace in WIN-066.detail_verdict_text) preempts both prongs of the defender pivot: (a) sign-non-discrimination (already partially addressed in prior text, now strengthened with Loeb's specific mechanism quote); (b) magnitude-disclaim retreat (newly addressed via the three-counter Defender pivot anticipated paragraph). After patch integration, the c3 hole #4 minor recommendation is fully closed and the ISS-1917 defender-pivot pattern is structurally rebutted in the page text.

scope_note: Single-patch defense expansion (vs EXP-295's 3-patch). Justified because the WIN-066 c3 review's other holes (citation, TLDR, range derivation) were already addressed by prior patches in current workspace head — only the defender-pivot rebuttal remained. Patch quality (kernel-of-truth acknowledgment + three-counter structure + Loeb mechanism quote) over patch count.
tldr: ISS-1917 (defense_survives=3) defender pivot — "we predict sign (NH>SH), not magnitude; sign-match is meaningful" — preempted by single-patch defender-pivot-anticipation paragraph in WIN-066.detail_verdict_text. Three-counter rebuttal: (1) sign is independently predicted by mainstream science from land-mass + aerosols (Loeb 2025 mechanism); (2) the dome cites +0.34 W/m²/decade specifically, so sign-only retreat contradicts its own quantitative claim; (3) without magnitude derivation, +0.34 coincidence is content-free.
